title: Palestinian-American Teenager Shot and Killed by Israeli Settlers in Mukhmas
detail: Nasrallah Abu Siyam, a Palestinian-American teenager, was shot and killed by Israeli settlers in the West Bank village of Mukhmas, northeast of Jerusalem — the second Palestinian-American killed by Israeli settlers in under a year. The killing drew heightened international attention given the victim's US citizenship. UN OCHA, Human Rights Watch, and the Palestinian Authority confirmed the incident. The shooting was part of the ongoing surge in settler violence: 26+ Palestinians killed in the West Bank in 2026 and 257 settler incidents documented in the three weeks since February 28, with only 3% of settler violence cases ever resulting in Israeli convictions per Yesh Din.

sources.name: UN OCHA — Humanitarian Situation Tracker, March 27, 2026
Al Jazeera — Palestinian-American teenager killed by Israeli settlers in West Bank, March 2026
WAFA — Palestinian Authority official news agency, March 27, 2026

title: Gaza Strikes Continue; Al-Aqsa Mosque Closed for Second Consecutive Friday
detail: Multiple Israeli airstrikes, shelling, and gunfire continued across Gaza, particularly in eastern North Gaza, Gaza City, Bureij, and Khan Younis per UNRWA Situation Report #214. Israeli authorities continued the closure of the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound and cancellation of Friday prayers for the second consecutive week, with the Israeli Civil Administration confirming the decision was tied to the ongoing US-Israel war on Iran. Gaza health system remained critically strained: 46% of essential medicines out of stock, 66% of medical consumables unavailable (WHO/Gaza MoH); 18,500+ patients requiring urgent medical evacuation for conditions untreatable in Gaza; only 42% of 677 health facilities operational (20 fully, 264 partially). Thirty-nine percent of school-age children were enrolled in any form of education.

sources.name: WHO / Gaza Ministry of Health — Health system status, March 2026
Times of Israel — Live Blog, March 27, 2026
